markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar’s repeated delays of Grand Theft Auto VI, most recently shifting the target to May 26, 2026, have created a narrow window that traders are now weighing against fast-moving entertainment and geopolitical developments. The studio’s emphasis on polish, consistent with past GTA campaigns, keeps expectations for additional updates high during Take-Two’s upcoming May earnings call, where any further postponement signals could reshape the timeline. This uncertainty fuels speculation around major music drops, such as anticipated Playboi Carti or Rihanna albums, alongside broader cultural milestones like a potential Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or even GPT-6 advancements. With pre-order chatter and rumored Trailer 3 activity already circulating, the market reflects how industry caution around quality control intersects with real-time pop culture momentum before the game finally arrives.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrial have driven the strong market consensus behind no additional prison time. The third trial on the Jessica Mann rape allegation ended in a mistrial on May 15 after jurors deadlocked 9–3, marking the second hung jury on that charge in under a year. With prosecutors now weighing whether to pursue a fourth proceeding, traders see limited near-term risk of further convictions that would extend his sentence. Weinstein remains incarcerated under his separate 16-year California conviction for sexual assault, yet ongoing appeals and reports of declining health continue to shape perceptions of eventual release timelines. Upcoming decisions on retrial or plea discussions represent the next potential catalysts for shifts in these implied probabilities.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes sit neck-and-neck in the 2026 Stanley Cup futures market because both clubs posted strong regular-season records with balanced forward depth, defensive pairings, and reliable goaltending that translate directly to playoff success. Recent conference standings and head-to-head results have kept their implied probabilities separated by only a slim margin, while the Vegas Golden Knights and Montreal Canadiens trail with credible but narrower paths driven by mid-season momentum and prospect development. This bunched positioning reflects comparable team strengths across conferences and the absence of decisive roster-altering injuries heading into the postseason stretch.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 NBA playoffs have reached the conference finals, where the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs are locked in a Western Conference showdown and the New York Knicks hold a narrow lead over the Cleveland Cavaliers in the East. Oklahoma City enters as defending champions with the league’s top regular-season record and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander earning his second straight MVP, yet the Spurs’ 1-0 series advantage—capped by Victor Wembanyama’s dominant 41-point, 24-rebound performance in double overtime—has kept both teams’ implied probabilities tightly bunched. New York’s overtime victory in Game 1 highlights its defensive identity and Jalen Brunson’s May surge, while Cleveland’s lower odds reflect a tougher path despite Donovan Mitchell’s heroics. These recent results and roster health factors continue to shape trader consensus around the remaining contenders.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tightly clustered implied probabilities among France, Spain, England, and the other top contenders stem from the exceptional depth across elite squads and recent international form heading into the expanded 48-team tournament. Spain’s status as reigning European champions, bolstered by standout performers like Lamine Yamal when fit, has kept them neck-and-neck with France, whose blend of experience, defensive organization, and attacking firepower centered on Kylian Mbappé continues to resonate with traders. England’s strong qualifying campaign and squad balance place them just behind, while Brazil and Argentina carry historical pedigree but face notable injury concerns that have tempered their positioning. This competitive balance, combined with the unpredictable nature of knockout football and favorable group draws for several favorites, has prevented any single nation from pulling clear in the wisdom-of-crowds pricing.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tightly clustered implied probabilities among France, Spain, England, and the other top contenders stem from the exceptional depth across elite squads and recent international form heading into the expanded 48-team tournament. Spain’s status as reigning European champions, bolstered by standout performers like Lamine Yamal when fit, has kept them neck-and-neck with France, whose blend of experience, defensive organization, and attacking firepower centered on Kylian Mbappé continues to resonate with traders. England’s strong qualifying campaign and squad balance place them just behind, while Brazil and Argentina carry historical pedigree but face notable injury concerns that have tempered their positioning. This competitive balance, combined with the unpredictable nature of knockout football and favorable group draws for several favorites, has prevented any single nation from pulling clear in the wisdom-of-crowds pricing.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's continued consolidation of authority within the Chinese Communist Party underpins trader expectations that he will remain in power through at least the end of 2026. Recent leadership adjustments in the Central Military Commission, including removals tied to corruption investigations, have been interpreted as efforts to install loyalists ahead of the 21st Party Congress in 2027 rather than signs of instability. Xi has maintained a visible role in directing the launch of the 15th Five-Year Plan for 2026-2030 and in high-level diplomatic engagements, including summits framing a stable U.S.-China relationship. With no designated successor, verified health concerns, or internal challenges reported, the market reflects structural barriers such as the absence of term limits and centralized control over personnel and security appointments. Late-breaking events like unforeseen health developments could still shift the outlook before resolution.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market reflects a fragmented field more than two years after the party's 2024 defeat, with Gavin Newsom holding the highest implied probability at 24.9 percent due to his executive record as California governor, national fundraising reach, and early positioning within party networks. Kamala Harris at 8.9 percent and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8.6 percent draw support from distinct bases, the former tied to prior national visibility and the latter to progressive grassroots momentum, while Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro trail as Senate and gubernatorial figures with swing-state appeal. Key differentiators include prior elected experience, donor infrastructure, and ideological alignment that could consolidate during the primary process. Upcoming midterm results, candidate announcements, and debate performances remain primary catalysts that traders monitor for shifts in consensus pricing.
